//: Copy to agenthook.config.json in your project dir (or run `agenthook init`).
//secrets: Secrets are ${ENV} refs, resolved from the shell or a .env beside this file,
//secrets2: so this file stays shareable. State lives centrally in ~/.agenthook/<name>.
name: myproject
//digest: Optional. true = after a clean author run, spawn an INDEPENDENT claude -p that
//digest2: digests the draft PR (repo `pr-digest` skill) into a plain-language sign-off card
//digest3: on the tracker + a technical concerns list on the PR. Omit/false to disable.

tracker.//: type selects the adapter; the rest is that tracker's config.
tracker.//github-type: type: github, repo: owner/repo, assigneeLogin: you,
tracker.//pipeline: Optional. When present, a task ENTERING a step's sourceSectionGid fires that
tracker.//pipeline2: step; on a clean exit the receiver moves it to successSectionGid (= the next
tracker.//pipeline3: step's source, so the move itself triggers the next step). createsWorktree makes
tracker.//pipeline4: the receiver own a shared git worktree across all steps; manual = no agent (entry
tracker.//pipeline5: only runs system actions like drainWorktree). Forward motion is all event-driven;
tracker.//pipeline6: `agenthook reconcile` is the only board poll and is user-explicit.

ingress.//: How the receiver is reachable. ngrok (managed tunnel) | manual/hosted (static URL).
ingress.//domain: optional reserved domain for a stable URL (omit = ephemeral)
ingress.//manual: For a hosted URL instead: { type: hosted, url: https://hook.you.com }
